Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>請實作</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-29</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
理解了。你要的是 drop-out 的**觸發點**前移到「按下 Spin（spin 開始）」那一刻，讓舊盤在等伺服器回應的空檔就先掉出去；預告與新盤進場仍等資料到位才做。

確認過呼叫鏈：`GameView.StartSpin()`（→`SlotReels.StartSpin()`）只被 `SpinState.OnEnter` 呼叫（unshow L32、MG/FG L38），所以把 drop-out 觸發放進 `StartSpin` 就能一次覆蓋三條路徑（且天然處在「按 Spin」時點）。

## 修正後的方案

### 新時序

| 時點 | 動作 |
|------|------|
| **按 Spin（spin 開始）** | `StartSpin()` 內凍結盤面 + **立即 `TriggerDropOut()`** → 舊盤開始掉出（與等待伺服器重疊）|
| **資料到位**（MG=收到 ack / FG=既有 RoundQueue） | `await 掉出完成` → `ShowBigAwardHint`（預告，蓋空盤）→ `SetNowRoundInfo`（新盤進場）|

### 改動清單（3 檔）

**① `SlotReels.StartSpin()`（`SlotReels.ts:860`）** — 在函式末端（`L956` 處）加 `this.TriggerDropOut();`，把掉出觸發拉回 spin 開始；同步改寫 `L955-956`、`L866-867` 註解（原本記錄「掉出移出 StartSpin、預告蓋舊盤」的設計意圖反轉）。

**② `SlotReels` 新增公開等待方法** — 供 drop-in 前等舊盤掉完：
```typescript
/** @ch 等待舊盤掉出完成（掉出已在 StartSpin 觸發）*/
public async WaitDropOutComplete(): Promise<void> {
    if (this.m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise) {
        await this.m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise;
    }
}
```

**③ `GameView.OnRecvSpinAck()`（`GameView.ts:1653/1677`）** — 移除 `L1677` 的 `TriggerDropOut()`（已移至 StartSpin）；把 `L1653` 預告改成「先等掉完、再預告、再進場」：
```typescript
await this.m_slotReels.WaitDropOutComplete();              // 等舊盤掉完
await this.ShowBigAwardHint(this.SpinAck.RoundQueue[0]);   // 預告（空盤上）
this.SetNowRoundInfo(this.SpinAck.RoundQueue[this.CurRoundIndex]); // 新盤進場
```
（FG 進入判斷、地Bar 狀態維持原位，與此三步無依賴。）

**④ `SpinState.OnEnter()` FG 路徑（`SpinState.ts:48-50`）** — 移除 `L49` 的 `TriggerDropOut()`；同樣改為等掉完→預告→進場，並更新 `L46-47` 註解。

### 因果鏈 / 風險

- **`TriggerDropOut` 移入 `StartSpin`**：`StartSpin` 在 `L871` 先把 promise 清 null，末端 `TriggerDropOut` 才建立 → 順序正確；冪等鍵（promise 是否存在）仍有效。
- **時序正確性**：drop-out 在 spin 開始建立 promise；資料到位後外層 `WaitDropOutComplete` 等它（ack 快於 1.5s 則等剩餘、慢於則瞬間通過），drop-in 內層再 `await` 同一已 resolved promise（瞬間，清 null 在內層 await 後）→ **無雙重消費、無 await-null 競態**。
- **unshow**：`OnEnter` 在 `StartSpin`(L32) 即觸發掉出，緊接 `OnRecvSpinAck`(L33) 走同一條等待→預告→進場，順序正確。
- **凍結**：`m_isInDropMode` 貫穿全程，預告期間空盤靜止。
- **延遲改善**：掉出與網路等待重疊，按鍵手感更即時（這正是你要的）。
- **⚠️ 待實機驗證**：MgOmen 路徑（`SlotReels.ts:1741` 時 SetPlateInfo 不觸發 drop-in）——現行 code 在 OnRecvSpinAck 本就無條件觸發掉出，故此改動只是**提早**觸發時點、不改變「是否掉出」；但 MgOmen 進場另有處理（`GameView.ts:1693`），需確認提早掉出不影響其表演。

要我直接照這個實作嗎？

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

- **凍結**：`m_isInDropMode` 貫穿全程，預告期間空盤靜止。
- **延遲改善**：掉出與網路等待重疊，按鍵手感更即時（這正是你要的）。
- **⚠️ 待實機驗證**：MgOmen 路徑（`SlotReels.ts:1741` 時 SetPlateInfo 不觸發 drop-in）——現行 code 在 OnRecvSpinAck 本就無條件觸發掉出，故此改動只是**提早**觸發時點、不改變「是否掉出」；但 MgOmen 進場另有處理（`GameView.ts:1693`），需確認提早掉出不影響其表演。

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Refactor slot reel drop-out animation timing to start immediately when Spin button is pressed, rather than waiting for server response</request>
  <investigated>Call chain analysis confirmed GameView.StartSpin() → SlotReels.StartSpin() is only invoked by SpinState.OnEnter (covering unshow L32, MG/FG L38 paths). Current timing flow examined: drop-out trigger currently happens in OnRecvSpinAck after server response arrives. Three code paths identified (unshow, main game, free game) that all funnel through the same StartSpin entry point.</investigated>
  <learned>Drop-out animation can overlap with server wait time by moving TriggerDropOut() call from OnRecvSpinAck into StartSpin(). The timing sequence should be: press Spin → freeze board + trigger drop-out immediately (parallel with server wait) → server data arrives → await drop-out complete → show big award hint (preview on empty board) → drop in new reels. The m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise provides natural synchronization point. MgOmen path at SlotReels.ts:1741 bypasses normal drop-in via SetPlateInfo, but since OnRecvSpinAck already unconditionally triggers drop-out in current code, moving the trigger earlier only changes timing, not whether drop-out occurs.</learned>
  <completed>Detailed implementation plan created covering 3 files with 4 specific changes: (1) SlotReels.StartSpin (L860/L956) add TriggerDropOut() call at function end, update comments, (2) Add public WaitDropOutComplete() method to SlotReels for external coordination, (3) GameView.OnRecvSpinAck (L1653/L1677) remove TriggerDropOut call and change to await-wait-preview-dropin sequence, (4) SpinState.OnEnter FG path (L48-50) remove TriggerDropOut and apply same await sequence. Risk analysis completed: timing correctness verified, promise lifecycle safe (cleared at L871 before recreation), no double-consumption race conditions, freeze state maintained throughout.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user confirmation to proceed with implementing the 4-file change set. User asked "要我直接照這個實作嗎？" (Should I implement this directly?). Once confirmed, will apply the changes to SlotReels.ts, GameView.ts, and SpinState.ts, then verify MgOmen path behavior doesn't break from earlier drop-out trigger timing.</next_steps>
  <notes>This refactoring improves perceived responsiveness by making drop-out animation happen during network latency rather than after. The architectural insight is that StartSpin() is the natural choke point for all three game modes, making it the ideal place to trigger animations that should happen "on button press" rather than "on data arrival". The promise-based synchronization ensures preview and drop-in still wait for both server data AND animation completion.</notes>
</summary>

return;\\n        }\\n\\n        const totalDuration = 1.5;\\n        const singleFallDuration = Math.min(0.4, totalDuration);\\n\\n        // 收集所有要掉落的 Node，並依指定順序排列：\\n        // 「第一列(col=0) 先全部掉完（從最後一顆開始）→ 再輪到第二列(col=1) → ...」\\n        // 也就是 col：左→右，row：下→上（同一列先掉完才換下一列）。\\n        const symbolNodes: Node[] = [];\\n        const middlePlateIndex = Game_Define.MIDDLE_PLATE_INDEX;\\n\\n        // 只處理中間盤面（掉落模式只有一個盤面）\\n        const plateColumns = this.m_allColumns[middlePlateIndex];\\n        if (plateColumns) {\\n            for (let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++) {\\n                const columnSymbol = plateColumns[col];\\n                if (!columnSymbol) {\\n                    continue;\\n                }\\n\\n                const nodeCount = columnSymbol.NodeCount }\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        if (symbolNodes.length === 0) {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        const fallDistance = this.m_viewSize.height + this.m_symbolSize.height;\\n        const stagger = 0; // 全部一起掉出盤面\\n\\n        const promises: Promise<void>[] = [];\\n        for (let i = 0; i < symbolNodes.length; i++) {\\n            const v3(startPos.x, startPos.y - fallDistance, startPos.z);\\n                const delayTime = i * stagger;\\n\\n                tween(symbolNode)\\n                    .delay(delayTime)\\n                    .to(singleFallDuration, { position: targetPos }, { easing: \\\"quadIn\\\" })\\n                    .call(resolve)\\n                    .start();\\n            });\\n            promises.push(promise);\\n        }\\n\\n        await Promise.all(promises);\\n\\n        // 動畫結束後，直接隱藏所有輪帶的 Symbol\\n        for (let plate = 0; plate < SHOW_COLUMNS; plate++) {\\n            const  /**\\n     * @ch 檢查盤面是否正確\\n     */\\n    CheckPlateInfo( expectedPlate?: protocol.ar2pq9Proto.IColumn[], context: string = \\\"旋停\\\" )\\n    {\\n        // 期望盤面：不傳 → 沿用 m_plateInfo（旋停舊行為）；傳入 → 用該 content.thinking:
The user is observing a primary session where an Edit operation was performed on a TypeScript file. Let me analyze what was done:

**File**: `SlotReels.ts` (slot reel system for a game)
**Working Directory**: `G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen`

**What Changed**: 
The edit modified comments in the `StartSpin()` method around line 866-867. The comment was updated to reflect a timing refinement:

**Old comment**:
```
// 修 R-1：StartSpin 即凍結轉輪（恢復舊版首幀凍結），使 StartSpin→TriggerDropOut 窗口舊盤面靜止，
// 預告蓋在靜止舊盤上；drop-in 原子解除才設回 false。
```

**New comment**:
```
// 修 R-1：StartSpin 即凍結轉輪（恢復舊版首幀凍結），凍結貫穿整個掉落序列，
// 預告期間空盤面（舊盤已掉出）保持靜止；drop-in 原子解除才設回 false。
```

**Translation and meaning**:
- Old: "StartSpin freezes the reel immediately (restoring old first-frame freeze), making the old board static during the StartSpin→TriggerDropOut window, preview displays on static old board"
- New: "StartSpin freezes the reel immediately (restoring old first-frame freeze), freeze spans the entire drop sequence, during preview the empty board (old board already dropped out) remains static"

This is a **documentation update** (change type) that clarifies the timing behavior. The key change is acknowledging that:
1. The freeze spans the "entire drop sequence" (not just StartSpin→TriggerDropOut window)
2. The preview now shows on an "empty board" (old board already dropped out), not on the "static old board"

This aligns with the earlier discussion about moving the drop-out trigger timing to happen earlier (at Spin button press) so the old board drops out during server wait time, and the preview shows on an empty board.

The freeze encompasses the complete drop sequence rather than a limited window. Meanwhile, the preview renders against a cleared board state—the previous board has already exited—instead of remaining visible over the static old board. These adjustments stem from shifting when the drop-out trigger fires to the Spin button press, allowing the old board to exit while waiting for the server response, leaving an empty canvas for the preview.

symbolNode.setPosition(targetPos);\\n                    resolve();\\n                })\\n                .start();\\n        });\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 開始落下所有Symbol\\n     * @param content.thinking:
This observation shows a significant change to the SlotReels.ts file. The developer is implementing the drop-out timing refactoring by:

1. Moving the TriggerDropOut() call into the StartSpin() method itself (line 957 in the new code)
2. Updating the comments to reflect this new architecture where:
   - Drop-out is triggered immediately when Spin button is pressed (at the end of StartSpin)
   - This allows old symbol drop-out to overlap with waiting for server acknowledgement (better responsiveness)
   - Preview and new board entry now happens after data arrives, with each path (normal game = OnRecvSpinAck, FG = SpinState) first calling WaitDropOutComplete() to wait for drop-out to finish

This is implementing the first part of the 4-file change plan described in the progress summary.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented drop-out animation trigger on spin button press in slot reel system</title>
  <subtitle>Drop-out animation now starts immediately when StartSpin is called, overlapping network wait time for improved responsiveness</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.StartSpin() now calls this.TriggerDropOut() at line 957 (end of method)</fact>
    <fact>Drop-out animation timing moved from post-server-response to immediate trigger on spin button press</fact>
    <fact>Old symbol drop-out now overlaps with server acknowledgement wait time instead of being sequential</fact>
    <fact>Preview and new board entry remain deferred until server data arrives</fact>
    <fact>Each execution path (OnRecvSpinAck for main game, SpinState for free game) will call WaitDropOutComplete() before preview/drop-in</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented the first phase of a slot reel drop-out timing refactoring. The TriggerDropOut() method call was moved into the StartSpin() method at line 957, enabling the old symbol drop-out animation to start immediately when the spin button is pressed rather than waiting for server acknowledgement. This architectural change allows the drop-out animation (approximately 1.5 seconds) to overlap with network latency, significantly improving perceived responsiveness. The updated comments clarify the new flow: spin button press triggers drop-out immediately, drop-out overlaps with server ack wait, then once data arrives each path (main game via OnRecvSpinAck, free game via SpinState) awaits WaitDropOutComplete() before showing preview and dropping in new symbols. This maintains visual correctness (preview shown on empty board after old symbols drop) while optimizing timing.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   User requested "請實作" (Please implement) to execute a slot reel drop-out animation timing refactoring that moves the trigger point from after server response to immediately on Spin button press.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Slot reel drop-out animation timing optimization
   - Overlapping animation with network latency for improved perceived responsiveness
   - Promise-based synchronization (m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise)
   - Freeze state management (m_isInDropMode)
   - Three game paths: unshow, main game (MG), and free game (FG)
   - TriggerDropOut() idempotency mechanism
   - WaitDropOutComplete() synchronization pattern

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **SlotReels.ts (Line 866-867 edit)**:
     - Updated freeze timing documentation
     - Changed comment from "StartSpin→TriggerDropOut 窗口舊盤面靜止，預告蓋在靜止舊盤上" to "凍結貫穿整個掉落序列，預告期間空盤面（舊盤已掉出）保持靜止"
     - Purpose: Clarify that freeze now spans entire sequence and preview shows on empty board
   
   - **SlotReels.ts (Line 952-959 edit)**:
     - Modified end of StartSpin() method
     - Added actual implementation code: `this.TriggerDropOut();`
     - Updated comments from explaining old architecture to new architecture
     - New comment explains: "按 Spin（spin 開始）即觸發舊盤面掉出——在此 StartSpin 末端觸發，使舊盤掉出與等待伺服器 ack 的空檔重疊（手感即時）"
     - Purpose: Implement immediate drop-out trigger to overlap with server wait time

4. Errors and Fixes:
   No errors encountered in the observed session.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified that StartSpin() is the natural choke point for all three game modes (unshow, MG, FG)
   - Determined that moving TriggerDropOut() to StartSpin() allows drop-out animation to overlap with network latency
   - Designed synchronization using WaitDropOutComplete() to ensure preview and drop-in still wait for both server data AND animation completion
   - Addressed MgOmen edge case: confirmed that moving trigger earlier only changes timing, not whether drop-out occurs

6. All User Messages:
   - "請實作" (Please implement)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Add public WaitDropOutComplete() method to SlotReels class
   - Modify GameView.OnRecvSpinAck() to remove TriggerDropOut() call and add wait-preview-dropin sequence
   - Modify SpinState.OnEnter() FG path to remove TriggerDropOut() call and add same wait sequence
   - Verify MgOmen path behavior after implementation

8. Current Work:
   Implementing the first step of a 4-step refactoring plan to move slot reel drop-out animation trigger timing. Just completed adding `this.TriggerDropOut();` call at the end of SlotReels.StartSpin() method (lines 952-959). This makes old symbols start dropping out immediately when Spin button is pressed, allowing the drop-out animation to overlap with server response time. The implementation is awaiting user confirmation to proceed with remaining 3 steps.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Wait for user confirmation on whether to proceed with implementing the remaining 3 changes:
   - Add WaitDropOutComplete() method
   - Update OnRecvSpinAck()  
   - Update SpinState.OnEnter()
   
   Claude's direct quote: "要我直接照這個實作嗎？" (Should I implement this directly?) - awaiting user response before proceeding.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\projects\C--Users-jiunchiwang--claude-mem-observer-sessions\0ff69228-1058-4846-81d3-3067ea34faeb.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.
